description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance, featuring a mix of earthy tones including ochre, tan, gray, and hints of white. The surface is uneven, with visible chips, rough patches, and some smoother worn areas, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. There are handwritten markings on one side, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The stone's form is asymmetrical, with one side broader and flatter, while the opposite side tapers to a narrower edge. Its texture appears coarse and granular, with some areas showing subtle mottling and color transitions. The overall impression is of a naturally weathered stone, likely sourced from an outdoor environment.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped stone with earthy ochre, tan, and gray tones, featuring rough and smooth patches and visible catalog markings.
description_view_0: The broadest side of the stone is visible, showing a mix of ochre, tan, and gray colors with a prominent rough patch and some handwritten markings.
description_view_1: A narrow, edge-on view highlights the stone's thickness and the handwritten cataloging text along the side.
description_view_2: The opposite broad side is shown, dominated by ochre and tan hues with less visible text and a smoother surface.
description_view_3: A tapered, pointed end of the stone is visible, emphasizing its asymmetrical, wedge-like shape and the transition of colors.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and color transitions with some natural veining; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with some smoother, worn areas, indicating moderate exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, but may contrast with paved or manicured urban surfaces.
